spec_match.rationale: The live simulator and copyable header examples now emit the Chrome 142 Use-As-Dictionary ttl=<seconds> parameter rather than the unrelated max-age parameter.

interactive.rationale: Four range sliders drive a two-bar comparison, savings state, live dictionary response, next request, and vN+1 response headers.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede, labels, note, code sample, and generated header all name ttl= as the newly added expiry lever.

edge_cases.rationale: The controls now cover ttl=0 instant expiry, perfect-match header-only payloads, high-change-rate fallback to plain brotli, and the Sec-Available-Dictionary/Sec-Dictionary-Id negotiation.

references.rationale: Links chromestatus.com/feature/6408307086589952 and the developer.chrome.com shared-dictionary-compression blog.

design_system.rationale: Uses shared CSS variables, removes inline style attributes from value readouts, keeps letter spacing neutral, and preserves a real interactive control surface.

summary: Resolved the bundle-delta critique by switching every generated Use-As-Dictionary dictionary lifetime from max-age= to ttl=, adding live request/response negotiation panels, and surfacing ttl=0, perfect-match, and high-change-rate fallback edge cases.
